title: gastrolobium ebracteolatum
intro: Gastrolobium ebracteolatum, also known as Poison Pea, is a toxic plant native to Western Australia, containing toxic compounds that make it hazardous if ingested. The plant has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, including its ability to inhibit the growth of cancer cells and its antimicrobial properties. In traditional medicine, Gastrolobium ebracteolatum is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Herbal preparations of the plant are not commonly used due to its toxicity, and handling it requires caution and expertise.
